questions.question: List two key characteristics of a Consortium blockchain. (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Consensus is controlled by a pre-selected set of nodes (a federation).
It offers a balance between the trustlessness of public chains and the efficiency of private chains.

questions.question: Compare and contrast Public and Private blockchains. Discuss their key features, including access control, efficiency, and typical use cases. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Access Control: Public is permissionless/open; Private is permissioned/restricted.
Transparency: Public offers full transparency; Private offers customizable/limited transparency.
Efficiency: Public has lower TPS due to large consensus mechanisms; Private has higher TPS due to fewer, known validators.
Governance: Public is decentralized; Private is centralized.
Use Cases: Public is for cryptocurrencies/dApps; Private is for internal business operations/auditing.
